data.content:
### Install Stripe Sync Engine

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/sync-engine/README.md

Installs the Stripe Sync Engine library and the Stripe SDK using npm, pnpm, or yarn.

```sh
npm install @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
# or
pnpm add @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
# or
yarn add @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Install Documentation Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/contributing.md

Installs the necessary Python dependencies for building and serving the project documentation using uv and mkdocs.

```shell
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
uv pip install -r docs/requirements_docs.txt
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe Sync Engine

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/typescript.md

Installs the Stripe Sync Engine library and the Stripe SDK using npm, pnpm, or yarn.

```sh
npm install @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
# or
pnpm add @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
# or
yarn add @supabase/stripe-sync-engine stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Daily/Weekly/Monthly Backfill Endpoints

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/fastify-app/README.md

Provides examples for triggering daily, weekly, or monthly data backfills from Stripe, with an option to specify the object type.

```APIDOC
POST /sync/daily

---

POST /sync/daily
body: {
  "object": "product"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Serve Documentation Locally

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/contributing.md

Serves the project documentation locally using mkdocs, allowing developers to preview changes before deployment. Requires the virtual environment to be activated.

```shell
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
mkdocs serve
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Documentation

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/contributing.md

Deploys the project documentation to GitHub Pages using the mkdocs gh-deploy command. This command is available for users with write access to the repository.

```shell
mkdocs gh-deploy
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize StripeSync

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/sync-engine/README.md

Initializes the StripeSync class with necessary configuration including database connection, Stripe API keys, and webhook secrets. Optionally accepts a logger instance.

```typescript
import { StripeSync } from '@supabase/stripe-sync-engine'

const sync = new StripeSync({
  databaseUrl: 'postgres://user:pass@host:port/db',
  stripeSecretKey: 'sk_test_...',
  stripeWebhookSecret: 'whsec_...',
  // logger: <a pino logger>
})

// Example: process a Stripe webhook
await sync.processWebhook(payload, signature)
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose Configuration

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/fastify-app/README.md

Sets up a Docker Compose environment including a PostgreSQL database and the Stripe Sync Fastify application, defining dependencies, ports, and environment variables for both services.

```yaml
version: '3'
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:17
    restart: always
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      POSTGRES_DB: postgres
    ports:
      - 5432:5432
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  stripe-sync:
    image: supabase/stripe-sync-fastify:latest
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/postgres?sslmode=disable&search_path=stripe
      STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: sk_test_...
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: whsec_...
      API_KEY: my-secret

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

--------------------------------

### Stripe Sync Engine - Overview

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/README.md

This snippet provides a high-level overview of the Stripe Sync Engine monorepo, detailing its purpose, components, and how it works to synchronize Stripe data with a Postgres database.

```Markdown
# Stripe Sync Engine Monorepo

This monorepo contains two packages for synchronizing your Stripe account with a Postgres database:

- [`@supabase/stripe-sync-engine`](./packages/sync-engine/README.md): A TypeScript library for syncing Stripe data to Postgres, designed for integration into your own Node.js backend or serverless environment.
- [`stripe-sync-fastify`](./packages/fastify-app/README.md): A Fastify-based server and Docker image that exposes a `/webhooks` endpoint for Stripe, providing a ready-to-run service for real-time Stripe-to-Postgres sync.

## Motivation

Sometimes you want to analyze your billing data using SQL. Even more importantly, you want to join your billing data to your product/business data.

This project synchronizes your Stripe account to a Postgres database. It can be a new database, or an existing Postgres database.

## How it works

- Creates a new schema `stripe` in a Postgres database, with tables & columns matching Stripe.
- Exposes a `/webhooks` endpoint that listens to any Stripe webhooks (via the Fastify app).
- Inserts/updates/deletes changes into the tables whenever there is a change to Stripe.

## Packages

- [Library: @supabase/stripe-sync-engine](./packages/sync-engine/README.md)
- [Docker/Server: supabase/stripe-sync-engine](./packages/fastify-app/README.md)

Each package has its own README with installation, configuration, and usage instructions.

## Supabase Edge Function

To deploy the sync-engine to a Supabase edge function, follow this [guide](./edge-function.md).
```

--------------------------------

### Run Database Migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/sync-engine/README.md

Executes database migrations for the Stripe Sync Engine. Requires the database connection string.

```typescript
import { runMigrations } from '@supabase/stripe-sync-engine'

await runMigrations({ databaseUrl: 'postgres://...' })
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize and Process Stripe Webhook

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/typescript.md

Initializes the StripeSync class with database and Stripe credentials and demonstrates processing a Stripe webhook payload.

```typescript
import { StripeSync } from '@supabase/stripe-sync-engine'

const sync = new StripeSync({
  databaseUrl: 'postgres://user:pass@host:port/db',
  stripeSecretKey: 'sk_test_...',
  stripeWebhookSecret: 'whsec_...',
  // logger: <a pino logger>
})

// Example: process a Stripe webhook
await sync.processWebhook(payload, signature)
```

--------------------------------

### API: Backfill Daily/Weekly/Monthly Data

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/docker.md

Provides alternative API routes for backfilling data based on specific timeframes (daily, weekly, monthly).

```APIDOC
POST /sync/daily

---

POST /sync/daily
body: {
  "object": "product"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Run Command

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/fastify-app/README.md

Runs the Stripe Sync Fastify application as a detached Docker container, configuring essential environment variables for database connection, Stripe API keys, and port mapping.

```sh
docker run -d \
  -e DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres \
  -e STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... \
  -e STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=... \
  -e API_KEY="my-secret" \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  supabase/stripe-sync-engine:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose Configuration

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/docker.md

Sets up a Docker Compose environment with PostgreSQL and the Stripe Sync Engine, configuring environment variables and dependencies.

```yaml
version: '3'
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:17
    restart: always
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
      POSTGRES_DB: postgres
    ports:
      - 5432:5432
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

  stripe-sync:
    image: supabase/stripe-sync-fastify:latest
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    ports:
      - 8080:8080
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/postgres?sslmode=disable&search_path=stripe
      STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: sk_test_...
      STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: whsec_...
      API_KEY: my-secret

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

--------------------------------

### Run Supabase Stripe Sync Engine Migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/edge-function.md

Executes database migrations for the Stripe Sync Engine using a provided database URL and schema. It's recommended to run these migrations manually, in CI, or locally once.

```typescript
import { runMigrations } from '@supabase/stripe-sync-engine'
;(async () => {
  await runMigrations({
    databaseUrl: 'postgresql://postgres:..@db.<ref>.supabase.co:5432/postgre',
    schema: 'stripe',
    logger: console,
  })
})()
```

--------------------------------

### Stripe Webhook Handling with Fastify

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/index.md

This snippet outlines the webhook support for the Stripe Sync Engine. It details which Stripe events are currently supported and processed by the Fastify application, indicating the status of each event type.

```Fastify
This project exposes a `/webhooks` endpoint that listens to any Stripe webhooks (via the Fastify app).
Inserts/updates/deletes changes into the tables whenever there is a change to Stripe.

Webhook Support:
- `balance.available`
- `charge.captured` 🟢
- `charge.expired` 🟢
- `charge.failed` 🟢
- `charge.pending` 🟢
- `charge.refunded` 🟢
- `charge.refund.updated` 🟡 - For updates on all refunds, listen to `refund.updated` instead
- `charge.succeeded` 🟢
- `charge.updated` 🟢
- `charge.dispute.closed` 🟢
- `charge.dispute.created` 🟢
- `charge.dispute.funds_reinstated` 🟢
- `charge.dispute.funds_withdrawn` 🟢
- `charge.dispute.updated` 🟢
- `checkout.session.async_payment_failed`
- `checkout.session.async_payment_succeeded`
- `checkout.session.completed`
- `credit_note.created` 🟢
- `credit_note.updated` 🟢
- `credit_note.voided` 🟢
- `customer.created` 🟢
- `customer.deleted` 🟢
- `customer.source.created`
- `customer.source.updated`
- `customer.subscription.created` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.deleted` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.paused` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.pending_update_applied` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.pending_update_expired` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.resumed` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.trial_will_end` 🟢
- `customer.subscription.updated` 🟢
- `customer.tax_id.created` 🟢
- `customer.tax_id.deleted` 🟢
- `customer.tax_id.updated` 🟢
- `customer.updated` 🟢
- `invoice.created` 🟢
- `invoice.deleted` 🟢
- `invoice.finalized` 🟢
- `invoice.finalization_failed` 🟢
- `invoice.marked_uncollectible` 🟢
- `invoice.paid` 🟢
- `invoice.payment_action_required` 🟢
- `invoice.payment_failed` 🟢
- `invoice.payment_succeeded` 🟢
- `invoice.sent` 🟢
- `invoice.upcoming` 🔴 - Event has no id and cannot be processed
- `invoice.updated` 🟢
- `invoice.overdue` 🟢
- `invoice.overpaid` 🟢
- `invoice.will_be_due` 🟢
- `invoice.voided` 🟢
- `issuing_authorization.request`
- `issuing_card.created`
- `issuing_cardholder.created`
- `payment_intent.amount_capturable_updated` 🟢
- `payment_intent.canceled` 🟢
- `payment_intent.created` 🟢
- `payment_intent.partially_refunded` 🟢
- `payment_intent.payment_failed` 🟢
- `payment_intent.processing` 🟢
- `payment_intent.requires_action` 🟢
- `payment_intent.succeeded` 🟢
- `payment_method.attached` 🟢
- `payment_method.automatically_updated` 🟢
- `payment_method.detached` 🟢
- `payment_method.updated` 🟢
- `plan.created` 🟢
- `plan.deleted` 🟢
- `plan.updated` 🟢
- `price.created` 🟢
- `price.deleted` 🟢
- `price.updated` 🟢
- `product.created` 🟢
- `product.deleted` 🟢
- `product.updated` 🟢
- `radar.early_fraud_warning.created` 🟢
- `radar.early_fraud_warning.updated` 🟢
- `refund.created` 🟢
- `refund.failed` 🟢
- `refund.updated` 🟢
- `review.opened` 🟢
- `review.closed` 🟢
- `setup_intent.canceled` 🟢
- `setup_intent.created` 🟢
- `setup_intent.requires_action` 🟢
- `setup_intent.setup_failed` 🟢
- `setup_intent.succeeded` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.aborted` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.canceled` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.completed` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.created` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.expiring` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.released` 🟢
- `subscription_schedule.updated` 🟢
```

--------------------------------

### Docker: Run Stripe Sync Engine Container

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/docker.md

Runs the Stripe Sync Engine Docker container with essential environment variables for database connection, Stripe keys, and port mapping.

```sh
docker run -d \
  -e DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres \
  -e STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_... \
  -e STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=... \
  -e API_KEY="my-secret" \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  supabase/stripe-sync-engine:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Backfill Stripe Data Endpoint

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/fastify-app/README.md

Demonstrates how to backfill Stripe data using the `/sync` endpoint, specifying the object type and a creation date range filter.

```APIDOC
POST /sync
body: {
  "object": "product",
  "created": {
    "gte": 1643872333
  }
}

- `object`: Specifies the Stripe object type to sync. Supported values include: all, charge, customer, dispute, invoice, payment_method, payment_intent, plan, product, setup_intent, subscription, early_fraud_warning, refund, credit_note, tax_id, subscription_schedules.
- `created`: A Stripe.RangeQueryParam object used to filter by creation date. Supports `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte` operators.
```

--------------------------------

### API: Backfill Stripe Data

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/docker.md

Initiates a backfill of Stripe data to Postgres. For large datasets (over 10,000 objects), consider a script with date filters.

```APIDOC
POST /sync
body: {
  "object": "product",
  "created": {
    "gte": 1643872333
  }
}

- `object`: Specifies the Stripe object type to sync (e.g., 'product', 'customer', 'invoice'). Supports all, charge, customer, dispute, invoice, payment_method, payment_intent, plan, price, product, setup_intent, subscription, early_fraud_warning, refund, credit_note, tax_id, subscription_schedules.
- `created`: Filters by creation date using Stripe's RangeQueryParam (supports gt, gte, lt, lte).
```

--------------------------------

### Run Database Migrations

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/typescript.md

Executes database migrations for the Stripe Sync Engine using the provided PostgreSQL connection string.

```typescript
import { runMigrations } from '@supabase/stripe-sync-engine'

await runMigrations({ databaseUrl: 'postgres://...' })
```

--------------------------------

### Set Supabase Secrets from .env File

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/edge-function.md

Loads Stripe and database credentials from a local .env file into Supabase secrets using the Supabase CLI.

```sh
supabase secrets set --env-file ./supabase/.env
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Pull Command

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/fastify-app/README.md

Pulls the latest Docker image for the Stripe Sync Fastify application.

```sh
docker pull supabase/stripe-sync-engine:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Backfill Stripe Data

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/packages/sync-engine/README.md

Performs a backfill of Stripe data into the database. Allows filtering by object type and creation date ranges. For large datasets, consider daily batching.

```typescript
await sync.syncBackfill({
  object: 'product',
  created: { gte: 1643872333 }, // Unix timestamp
})
```

--------------------------------

### Docker: Pull Stripe Sync Engine Image

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/docker.md

Pulls the latest Docker image for the Stripe Sync Engine.

```sh
docker pull supabase/stripe-sync-engine:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize and Process Stripe Webhooks with Supabase Edge Functions

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/edge-function.md

Sets up the StripeSync engine within a Supabase Edge Function to process incoming Stripe webhooks. It loads secrets from environment variables and handles webhook signature verification.

```typescript
// Setup type definitions for built-in Supabase Runtime APIs
import 'jsr:@supabase/functions-js/edge-runtime.d.ts'
import { StripeSync } from 'npm:@supabase/stripe-sync-engine@0.37.2'

// Load secrets from environment variables
const databaseUrl = Deno.env.get('DATABASE_URL')!
const stripeWebhookSecret = Deno.env.get('STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET')!
const stripeSecretKey = Deno.env.get('STRIPE_SECRET_KEY')!

// Initialize StripeSync
const stripeSync = new StripeSync({
  databaseUrl,
  stripeWebhookSecret,
  stripeSecretKey,
  backfillRelatedEntities: false,
  autoExpandLists: true,
  maxPostgresConnections: 5,
})

Deno.serve(async (req) => {
  // Extract raw body as Uint8Array (buffer)
  const rawBody = new Uint8Array(await req.arrayBuffer())

  const stripeSignature = req.headers.get('stripe-signature')

  await stripeSync.processWebhook(rawBody, stripeSignature)

  return new Response(null, {
    status: 202,
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  })
})
```

--------------------------------

### Postgres Schema Creation for Stripe Data

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/index.md

This snippet describes the database schema management performed by the Stripe Sync Engine. It details the creation of a new schema named 'stripe' within a PostgreSQL database, including the structure of tables and columns to mirror Stripe's data model.

```SQL
Creates a new schema `stripe` in a Postgres database, with tables & columns matching Stripe.
```

--------------------------------

### Backfill Stripe Data

Source: https://github.com/supabase/stripe-sync-engine/blob/main/docs/typescript.md

Performs a backfill of Stripe data, allowing filtering by object type and creation date range. Supports various Stripe objects and date range parameters.

```typescript
await sync.syncBackfill({
  object: 'product',
  created: { gte: 1643872333 }, // Unix timestamp
})
```
